reviews.review_title: This game is a ton of fun! Get it!
reviews.review_description:
I’ll start by saying I’m a big Ninja Warrior fan, but also an adult and I don’t typically like gorilla tag locomotion/games. And judging by the trailer and graphics, the lack of exposure/marketing, I didn’t have high hopes for it.  

But this game is solid. It’s incredibly fun and challenging. They’ve created a really cool atmosphere with the multiplayer and register system. I can see they want to support real competitions some time. It’s awesome to watch people progress and get closer each time, then take your turn and hear people cheering you on. 

And man… it feels so good to have ten seconds left and hear that warning sound, and end up hitting the button with not an extra second to spare. 

My only wish was that the obstacles were actually physics based, but some of them get close to it using controller motions to sort of influence the object that’s moving. For the most part they feel pretty good. I can see why they didn’t go with physics, it would suck to have some physics bug take you out. 

Overall, for the price, just buy it! It’s worth it.

developer_response.body:
Hi S-P-A-C-E-D,

Thank you for the review! We're really happy you're enjoying the game, especially as a Ninja Warrior fan! We've put a lot of effort into it and have worked closely with the original show producers to make a game that's not only fun and engaging, but also a nostalgic and faithful nod to fans of the show. More stages and updates are on the way, so we hope you continue to enjoy and challenge the course by yourself and with others on your way to Total Victory!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review, we're happy you're enjoying the game! The team put a lot of effort into the authenticity and feeling of the game, so we're happy to hear your kind words.

reviews.review_description: Good game but with over used monkey movement

1. It looks like this game functions well as intended, though I didn't go beyond the tutorial because when you finish it, you are in a stadium with other users. Immediately. I didn't see a way to do purely single user, though the description says it can be played that way. So a star off for that.

2. "Moderate" for intensity is flexible, but if you get motion sickness, this one is probably not for you. The developers were transparent about it, though. All motion is done with your hands, you are close to the ground. No stars off for that, though, it's the nature of the game.

developer_response.body:
Hi Dawn Monkey,

Thanks for the review! We're happy to hear you're enjoying the game! We can help you out with your first point: 

You can create a private Stadium with your own code and hop into it immediately if you'd like a true single player experience.

reviews.review_description: I've had my eye on this game since i saw it on the VR games showcase last November. When i saw it released it was not available in my country yet. Then when i can finally get my hands on it... Its 10 dollars. Now after i got a bit of money i considered buying it, but i wanted to make sure that the playerbase was still active because i knew the game was pretty contentless and people would quit after a while. So i got on the Discord and asked if the playerbase was still active. A community leader reached out and said the game still was active so i finally decided to buy it. And when i start playing i play the game with the clunkiest movement i have ever seen. When you run in the game, the games gravity pulls you back so you dont jump too far, which is stupid for a game all about going fast. There are these poles you have to jump and grab. But when i try to jump off one of those poles the jump is completely misaligned. Its so annoying that the only reason why failing stages is because of the badly designed pole jumps. And remember when i was told the game had an active playerbase? Guess what the first thing was that happened after i finished the tutorial? I see. One. Single. Person. In the lobby. Thats it! Then i try to play one of the stages and NO ONE was in the stages at all. The biggest lobby i got into was a lobby of 3 players in total. I asked anyone i could find why the game was dead and if i was lied to and of course if it was the wrong time to go online because of timezones and they said that this is how big the lobbies get. And last thing i want to talk about is the final stage or stage 4. When i beat all the other levels and got to it i failed on the first obstacle because of how weird and janky the movement was and after that guess what happened? I was forced to go back and play all previous levels to play level 4 again. The only good thing i can say about this game is how linear the levels are.
it's a good game it just came out it's only four stages i beated the last day just hard but i beat it twice anyways this game is peak
Very fun. It felt like I was on ninja warrior and the physics are great. I definitely recommend it.
